title: Data Sharing Docs Source Metadata Batch 2
content_html:
Batch 2: Source Metadata Read API

This is the delivery specification for Batch 2 in Data Sharing Docs Source Metadata Tasks.

Batch 2: Source Metadata Read API

Summary: Add the Docs Viewer-owned source metadata helper API that Data Sharing can consume without reading generated publication artifacts.

ID status action
2.1 planned Create or extend a Docs Viewer service module that loads configured docs scopes and parses source docs into canonical-equivalent metadata records for every configured scope.
2.2 planned Reuse shared Docs Viewer front matter parsing, doc-id validation, parent validation, scope config, URL construction, doc-link/media rendering, and content text extraction behavior instead of duplicating builder logic.
2.3 planned Include all locked Batch 1 record fields, including published, without changing current Data Sharing selection behavior.
2.4 planned Add source rendering or source-text extraction helpers so export flows no longer need generated by-id payloads for headings or source text.
2.5 planned Implement missing-source, duplicate-doc-id, unresolved-parent, and rendering-error behavior exactly as locked in Batch 1.
2.6 planned Ensure helper inputs are config-driven by scope id and do not branch on library, analysis, or other scope-specific names.
2.7 planned Add focused read-helper tests for record fields, all configured scope coverage, duplicate handling, missing source handling, parent/title derivation, content text length, headings/source-text extraction, and path safety.
2.8 planned Add negative tests proving the helper does not require public flat indexes, manage/local generated indexes, generated by-id payloads, search payloads, recently-added payloads, or generated metadata JSON.
2.9 planned Run focused Python syntax and pytest checks for changed helper and test files, then record results and handoff notes.

Steer for these tasks

  • This batch creates the source-read capability only; it does not migrate Data Sharing consumers yet except where tests need direct helper coverage.
  • see contract/audit in batch 1.
  • Do not add metadata-index.json, tooling-index.json, or any other generated metadata output.
  • Do not update scope lifecycle generated-output manifests because this request adds no generated data artifact.
  • Keep helper APIs narrow and shaped around current Data Sharing workflows.

Deliverables

  • Docs Viewer-owned source metadata helper API.
  • Source rendering/source-text helper for export use.
  • Focused helper tests and negative generated-artifact dependency tests.
  • Batch 3 handoff listing exact consumer call sites to migrate.

Implementation and policy guidance

  • Use structured source parsing and shared Docs Viewer rendering helpers.
  • Keep filesystem path knowledge inside Docs Viewer-owned helpers.
  • Generated publication artifacts are not valid inputs for this helper.
  • Do not preserve old read paths as fallback behavior.

Proposed verification set

  • $HOME/miniconda3/bin/python3 -m py_compile for changed helper and test files.
  • Focused pytest for the new source metadata helper tests.
  • git diff --check.
  • No generated writes.

completed verification

  • Not started.

follow-on tasks

  • Not started.

task close

  • Add a handoff note to Batch 3.
  • Set this document and the tracker row to done when the helper API and tests are in place.
